heaviest_rainfall.status: Ranked 7th for total rainfall, today's storm had precipitation totalling 19.92mm, falling at 5.4mm per hour at times during the 20.4 hour long storm.  47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 29 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (203°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 7.0°C), outside temperature (up 6.3°C), and inside temperature (up 1.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 2.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 502 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 1.6 mm), cloud levels (up 327 m), feels like temperature (down 2.6°C), and outside temperature (down 2.4°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 501.9m lower than yesterday, 274.5m lower than the past week, 327.0m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 854m minimum and 1568m maximum, with an average of 614.0m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 888.5m and 941.0m.
cloud levels.summary: Low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, air moisture should feel ok and near calm conditions. Light to moderate rain is forecast, low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The current weather conditions are not suitable as it is raining.
